acceptanceCriteria: A versioned promotion artifact schema (rigor.test-integrity-promotion.v1) records, per promoted signal: signal id, detector version and evaluated-candidate set, empirical evidence from shadow operation with explicit numerators and denominators (evaluated, fired, human-classified true-positive/false-positive/uncertain counts, reported per detector-version stratum and never pooled across a detector or candidate-set change), the promotion criteria applied, the assigned enforcement level (stop, review, or advisory), an explicit policy/schema version binding, rollback conditions, and a human approval declaration recorded as unattested local evidence

Promotion criteria defaults are defined, conservative, and documented: a minimum evaluated-sample denominator, a minimum count of human-classified fired observations, and a maximum acceptable false-positive proportion per enforcement level, such that the currently recorded shadow dataset (4 events, TI-05..TI-09 evaluated=4, fired=0, humanClassified=0) qualifies zero signals; the shipped default promoted set is empty and all existing behavior is backward-compatible

A deterministic promotion-gate evaluator validates any promotion record against its cited evidence and refuses to treat as active any record whose evidence does not meet the promotion criteria, whose detector version or evaluated-candidate set does not match the current detectors, whose schema/policy version binding does not match, or whose required linked replay report is absent; every enforced signal therefore necessarily cites empirical evidence from shadow operation

Enforcement distinguishes immediate stop, required human review, and advisory warning as distinct recorded outcomes, and with no active promotion records the verification, attempt, review, release-check, and CI code paths behave exactly as before with all pre-existing tests passing unmodified

Enforcement cannot be bypassed by deleting tests/checks or altering the signal or promotion configuration in the same unapproved change: the evaluator detects when the change under evaluation itself modifies promotion records, signal configuration, or the enforcement implementation, and in that case escalates to the stricter outcome (at minimum required human review) instead of applying the modified configuration

A human waiver is preserved as explicit reviewed evidence: a versioned waiver artifact recorded beside the enforcement outcome downgrades a specific fired enforcement to recorded-and-waived without deleting or hiding the original outcome, is append-only, and is labeled a human-reported declaration, never attested identity

A deterministic replay command re-evaluates recorded shadow test-integrity events against a proposed promotion record and reports what would have fired at each enforcement level with explicit denominators (shadow comparison), and a promotion record is not treated as active without a linked replay report generated before enablement

Replay fixtures cover at least: a qualifying promotion that activates, a non-qualifying promotion refused for insufficient evidence, a waiver applied to a fired enforcement, a rollback (deactivation) of a previously active promotion, and a false-positive-dominated sample that is refused promotion

docs/test-integrity.md documents the promotion criteria, enforcement levels, rollback conditions, waiver semantics, version binding, the fact that no signal is promoted today for lack of empirical evidence, and the remaining semantic blind spots, without weakening the what-must-never-be-advertised section
dist/rigor.cjs is rebuilt from the final source and committed, and .rigor/rigor-ci.cjs is updated only as a byte-identical copy of the rebuilt dist/rigor.cjs so the ci-bundle drift check passes

The recorded required check git diff --check passes; npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict are rerun by the orchestrator and their results reported in the pull request
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
